title: Go 1.26 and amqp091-go 1.12 Client Contract · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for go 1.26 and amqp091-go 1.12 client contract and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a reviewed Go client API and lifecycle contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Go 1.26.5, amqp091-go 1.12.0, AMQP 0-9-1, RabbitMQ 4.3.2, and the tested operating targets as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Classify amqp091-go methods, synchronous errors, notification channels, delivery channels, contexts, and confirmation streams by ownership and completion meaning.
Treat Delivery fields, properties, headers, and body bytes as untrusted data and validate decoded Go values before domain use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Runtime Client Version · changed case · Quiz Runtime Client Version
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 1254 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate delivered bytes and amqp091 notification ownership without mistaking Go types for validation; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Go 1.26.5, amqp091-go 1.12.0, AMQP 0-9-1, RabbitMQ 4.3.2, and the tested operating targets as one reviewed compatibility contract.
